name: The Arc
description: The largest national community-based organization advocating for and with people with intellectual and developmental disabilities.

name: World Institute on Disability - Accessibility Resources
description: Accessibility and universal design resources for creating inclusive environments.

name: National Parks Disability Access Pass
description: Free lifetime access pass for U.S. citizens with permanent disabilities to national parks and federal recreation sites.

name: CCMOO Clothing
description: Adaptive clothing including large body suits and g-tube access clothing for children with special needs.

name: CBO Baby
description: Large body suits and adaptive clothing for babies and children.

name: Billy Footwear
description: Adaptive shoes with zip-around design for easy on/off, designed for individuals with disabilities.

name: Feeding Tube Awareness - Adaptive Clothing Guide
description: Curated list of adaptive clothing options for individuals with feeding tubes.

name: TED Talks on Disability
description: Collection of inspiring TED Talks focused on disability, inclusion, and advocacy.

name: SBSK (Special Books by Special Kids)
description: Non-profit that tells disability and special needs stories to promote understanding and acceptance.

name: Early Childhood Technical Assistance Center (ECTA)
description: National resource center for early intervention and early childhood special education programs.

name: CDC - Learn the Signs. Act Early.
description: Free developmental milestone resources, checklists, and tips for parents to track their child's development from 2 months to 5 years.

name: PACER Center - Transition Resources
description: Comprehensive transition planning resources for youth with disabilities moving from school to adult life.

name: Think College
description: National resource for college options for people with intellectual disabilities, including program search and planning tools.

name: Social Security Disability Benefits (SSI/SSDI)
description: Federal disability benefits including Supplemental Security Income (SSI) and Social Security Disability Insurance (SSDI).

name: ABLE National Resource Center
description: Information about ABLE accounts — tax-advantaged savings accounts for individuals with disabilities.

name: National Disability Rights Network
description: Largest provider of legally based advocacy services for people with disabilities in the US.

name: Family Voices
description: National network helping families navigate healthcare and insurance systems for children with special health care needs.

name: Special Needs Alliance
description: National network of attorneys specializing in special needs trusts, guardianship, estate planning, and benefits preservation.

name: NAMI - National Alliance on Mental Illness
description: Mental health support, education, and advocacy including support groups for caregivers.

name: Caregiver Action Network
description: Support and resources specifically for family caregivers, including mental health resources and peer support.

name: HUD - Fair Housing & Disability
description: Federal resources on housing rights for people with disabilities, reasonable accommodations, and accessibility requirements.

name: Finding Disability-Friendly Doctors
description: Guide to finding medical providers who are experienced with and accessible to patients with disabilities.

name: American Camp Association - Find a Camp
description: Searchable database of accredited camps including camps for children with special needs and disabilities.

name: Sibling Support Project (Sibshops)
description: National program providing peer support for brothers and sisters of people with special needs. Includes Sibshops workshops.

name: Sibling Leadership Network
description: National network providing information, support, and tools for siblings of individuals with disabilities.

name: Assistive Technology Act Programs
description: National network providing AT information, device demonstrations, and lending programs in every state.

name: AbleData
description: National database of assistive technology products with over 40,000 products for people with disabilities.

name: Closing the Gap
description: Resource for assistive technology in education and employment for individuals with disabilities.

name: Special Olympics
description: World's largest sports organization for children and adults with intellectual disabilities — year-round training and competitions.

name: Move United (formerly DSUSA)
description: Adaptive sports programs for youth and adults with physical disabilities including skiing, cycling, kayaking, and more.

name: Miracle League
description: Adaptive baseball leagues for children with disabilities, with rubberized fields designed for wheelchairs and walkers.

name: Ready.gov - People with Disabilities
description: Federal emergency preparedness resources specifically for individuals with disabilities and their caregivers.

name: FEMA - Disability & Access
description: FEMA resources for emergency planning and disaster response for people with disabilities.

name: Rebuilding Together
description: Non-profit providing free home modifications and accessibility improvements for people with disabilities.

name: CAPS - Certified Aging-in-Place Specialist
description: Find certified professionals trained in accessible home design and modifications for people with disabilities.

name: Target - Cat & Jack Adaptive
description: Adaptive clothing line for kids featuring sensory-friendly options, seated-wear designs, abdominal access for feeding tubes, and easy-on/off shoes.

name: Target - Adaptive Backpacks & Gear
description: Wheelchair-compatible backpacks and adaptive gear designed for individuals who use mobility devices.

name: Tommy Hilfiger Adaptive
description: Mainstream fashion brand with adaptive clothing line featuring magnetic buttons, adjustable hems, and seated-wear designs for adults and children.

name: Nike FlyEase
description: Nike's hands-free, easy-entry shoe technology designed for people with disabilities. Features flip-back heels and zippered openings.

name: Zappos Adaptive
description: Curated collection of adaptive clothing, shoes, and accessories from multiple brands. Includes single-shoe purchasing for amputees.

name: Kohl's Adaptive Clothing
description: Adaptive clothing options including easy-dress styles, sensory-friendly fabrics, and wheelchair-friendly designs.

name: Kozie Clothes
description: Sensory-friendly clothing with tagless designs, flat seams, and soft fabrics for children with sensory processing challenges.

name: Downs Designs
description: Jeans and pants designed specifically for individuals with Down syndrome, accounting for body proportions and ease of dressing.

name: Juniper Adaptive
description: Adaptive clothing designed by occupational therapists for children with disabilities, featuring easy-dress openings and sensory-friendly fabrics.

name: Epilepsy Foundation
description: Leading national nonprofit supporting people with epilepsy and seizure disorders through education, advocacy, research funding, and community programs.

name: National Down Syndrome Society (NDSS)
description: Leading human rights organization for people with Down syndrome, providing advocacy, education, research, and community programs.

name: National Down Syndrome Congress
description: Provides information, advocacy, and support to families, individuals, and professionals involved with Down syndrome. Hosts annual convention.

name: Tim Tebow Foundation - Night to Shine
description: Hosts Night to Shine, a worldwide prom experience for people with special needs ages 14+. Events held at churches in all 50 states and internationally.

name: Colin Farrell Foundation
description: Founded by actor Colin Farrell, supports organizations and programs serving individuals with intellectual disabilities, inspired by his son who has Angelman syndrome.

name: United Cerebral Palsy (UCP)
description: National organization advancing the independence and inclusion of people with cerebral palsy and other disabilities through programs and advocacy.

name: Autism Speaks
description: Autism advocacy organization providing resources, research funding, and community support for individuals with autism and their families.

name: Muscular Dystrophy Association (MDA)
description: Supports people with muscular dystrophy and related neuromuscular diseases through research, care, and advocacy. Includes MDA Summer Camp.

name: Spina Bifida Association
description: National organization serving individuals with spina bifida through education, advocacy, research, and community programs.

name: National Organization for Rare Disorders (NORD)
description: Patient advocacy organization for individuals with rare diseases and disabilities. Provides financial assistance programs and a rare disease database.

name: Easterseals
description: Leading nonprofit providing services for people with disabilities including early intervention, therapy, employment training, camping, and adult day programs.

name: Best Buddies International
description: Creates one-to-one friendships, employment opportunities, and leadership development for people with intellectual and developmental disabilities.

name: GRIT Freedom Chair
description: Leveraged Freedom Chair (LFC) — an all-terrain wheelchair designed for active use on trails, grass, gravel, and sand. Designed at MIT.

name: Firefly by Leckey
description: Innovative wheelchair accessories including the Upsee (standing mobility device for children) and GoTo Seat (postural support). Helps children with disabilities participate in everyday activities.

name: Aquatrek
description: Beach and water wheelchairs designed for sand, surf, and pool access. Helps individuals with mobility challenges enjoy aquatic environments.

name: Frog Mobility LLC
description: Designs and manufactures innovative pediatric mobility devices including standing frames and gait trainers for children with disabilities.

name: BraunAbility
description: World's leading manufacturer of wheelchair accessible vehicles and wheelchair lifts. Offers converted minivans, SUVs, and commercial vehicles plus vehicle modification services.

name: BraunAbility Turny Evo
description: Powered rotating and lowering car seat that swivels out of the vehicle and lowers to a comfortable transfer height. Compatible with many vehicle models.

name: Huckleberry Hiking
description: Adaptive hiking carriers and trail chairs that enable people with disabilities to experience hiking trails and outdoor adventures with family and friends.

name: Charlie Foundation
description: Provides information about ketogenic diet therapies and other dietary treatments for epilepsy, neurological disorders, and select cancers. Founded in honor of Charlie Abrahams.

name: Keto Hope Foundation
description: Supports families using ketogenic diet therapy for seizure management. Provides education, resources, and community for families navigating dietary treatments for epilepsy.

name: MakeGood 3D Printed Wheelchair
description: Uses 3D printing technology to create custom, affordable wheelchairs and adaptive equipment. Innovative approach to personalized mobility solutions.

name: Apple - Accessibility
description: Apple builds accessibility into every product and regularly highlights disability inclusion through campaigns like 'I'm Not Remarkable' (2025) — a musical celebration of disabled individuals thriving in everyday life, including going to college.

name: Accessible Play VA
description: Instagram account highlighting accessible playgrounds, parks, and play spaces in Virginia. Reviews and rates play areas for wheelchair accessibility and inclusive design.

name: Amanda Griffith Atkins
description: Author of 'How You Can Handle More Than You Can Handle' and caregiver advocate. Shares disability parenting experiences, practical advice, and inspiration on Instagram.

name: Madison Tevlin
description: Disability advocate and content creator with Down syndrome. Shares her life, accomplishments, and advocacy work to challenge stereotypes and promote inclusion.

name: Magical Mendy
description: Instagram influencer sharing disability life, humor, and advocacy. Creates content that normalizes disability and celebrates accessible living.

name: Accessible Adventures
description: Instagram account documenting accessible travel, outdoor adventures, and recreation for people with disabilities. Reviews destinations and shares tips for accessible travel planning.

name: How You Can Handle More Than You Can Handle
description: By Amanda Griffith Atkins. A caregiver's guide offering practical strategies, encouragement, and real-life wisdom for parents navigating the challenges of raising a child with disabilities.

name: Able Fables
description: Children's book series celebrating disability and inclusion through engaging stories. Teaches kids about different abilities and the value of diversity in an age-appropriate way.

name: Let's Go Play! Inclusion
description: Children's book promoting inclusive play and helping young readers understand that all children — regardless of ability — deserve to play together.

name: This Beach Is Loud!
description: By Samantha Cotterill. A children's picture book showing how going to the beach can be overwhelming to the senses, combining storytelling with practical calming strategies. Great for kids with sensory processing differences.

name: Ian's Walk: A Story About Autism
description: By Laurie Lears. A picture book told from a sister's perspective about her brother Ian who has autism. Shows how he experiences the world differently through his senses.

name: Special Children, Challenged Parents
description: By Robert Naseef, Ph.D. Explores the struggles and rewards of raising a child with a disability. Offers professional guidance combined with personal experience as a father of a son with autism.

name: Differently Wired: Raising an Exceptional Child in a Conventional World
description: By Deborah Reber. A guide for parents raising children with ADHD, autism, giftedness, learning disabilities, and other neurological differences. Offers 18 paradigm shifts for parenting differently wired kids.

name: No More Meltdowns
description: By Jed Baker, Ph.D. Practical positive strategies for managing and preventing out-of-control behavior in children with autism, ADHD, and sensory processing challenges.

name: Everything No One Tells You About Parenting a Disabled Child
description: A comprehensive guide covering medical issues, insurance, financial planning, disability rights, and education plans. An actionable roadmap for newly diagnosed families.

name: You Will Dream New Dreams
description: A collection of inspiring personal stories by real parents who have experienced the hardships and joys of raising a child with a disability. Offers hope and connection.

name: The Sibling Survival Guide
description: By Don Meyer and Emily Holl. Written for siblings of people with disabilities, this guide addresses the unique joys and challenges of growing up with a brother or sister who has special needs.

name: Views from Our Shoes: Growing Up with a Brother or Sister with Special Needs
description: By Donald Meyer. Forty-five siblings share their experiences growing up with a brother or sister with a disability. Written by kids for kids ages 4-10.

name: The Universal Design for Learning Guidelines
description: Free resource from CAST providing evidence-based framework for designing flexible learning experiences that accommodate individual learning differences. Essential for IEP advocacy.

name: The Arc of Northern Virginia
description: Chapter of The Arc advocating for and serving people with intellectual and developmental disabilities. Provides information, referrals, advocacy support, and community programs.

name: The Arc of the United States
description: The nation's largest community-based organization for people with intellectual and developmental disabilities. Provides advocacy, resources, education, and support through 600+ state and local chapters.

name: Microsoft Accessibility
description: Microsoft's accessibility initiative includes the Xbox Adaptive Controller, built-in Windows accessibility features, and AI for Accessibility grant program. Committed to empowering people with disabilities through technology.

name: Tommy Hilfiger Adaptive
description: Adaptive clothing line featuring magnetic buttons, adjustable hems, and seated-wear designs. One of the first major fashion brands to create a dedicated adaptive collection for adults and children.

name: Zappos Adaptive
description: Zappos Adaptive collection features shoes and clothing designed for people with disabilities, including easy-on/easy-off shoes, single shoes, and adaptive apparel from major brands.

name: Spin the Globe - Sylvia Longmire
description: Award-winning wheelchair travel blogger and content creator. Sylvia shares accessible travel adventures, destination reviews, and tips for traveling in a power wheelchair. Over 130 travel videos produced.

name: Molly Burke
description: Blind motivational speaker, model, and content creator sharing her experience with vision loss. Advocates for accessibility and inclusion with millions of followers across platforms.

name: Shane Burcaw
description: Author and content creator with spinal muscular atrophy. Shane and his wife Hannah create content about disability, relationships, and daily life, challenging stereotypes with humor and honesty.

name: Aaron Philip
description: First Black transgender wheelchair-using model signed to a major modeling agency. Advocates for disability representation in fashion and media.

name: Abilities Expo
description: The nation's largest disability-focused expo, held in 7+ U.S. cities annually. Features hundreds of exhibitors showcasing adaptive equipment, assistive technology, vehicle modifications, recreation, and services. Free admission.

name: Permobil
description: Leading manufacturer of power wheelchairs, manual wheelchairs, and seating and positioning solutions. Also offers wheelchair accessible vehicle solutions and power assist devices.

name: Sunrise Medical
description: Global manufacturer of manual and powered wheelchairs (Quickie, Zippie brands), mobility scooters, and seating systems. Wide range of pediatric through adult mobility solutions.

name: Tobii Dynavox
description: Leading manufacturer of augmentative and alternative communication (AAC) devices and eye-tracking technology. Helps people with speech and communication challenges express themselves.

name: Numotion
description: Largest provider of Complex Rehab Technology (CRT) in the United States. Provides custom wheelchairs, seating, and mobility solutions with certified rehab technology professionals.

name: Rifton Equipment
description: Manufactures adaptive equipment for children and adults with disabilities, including gait trainers, standers, bath and toileting aids, and activity chairs. Known for high-quality pediatric equipment.

name: ABLE National Resource Center
description: Information hub for ABLE accounts — tax-advantaged savings accounts for people with disabilities under the ABLE Act. Compare state plans, learn eligibility rules, and open an account.

name: The Arc's Wings for Autism/All
description: Airport rehearsal program helping individuals with autism and intellectual disabilities practice air travel in a stress-free environment. Families walk through check-in, security, and boarding without actually flying.

name: Sibling Leadership Network
description: National network connecting siblings of people with disabilities for peer support, information, and advocacy. Provides resources, events, and state-level sibling networks.

name: MobilityWorks
description: Sells and services wheelchair-accessible vehicles nationwide. Offers van conversions, hand controls, wheelchair lifts, turning seats, and other vehicle modifications with locations across the U.S.

name: VMI - Vantage Mobility International
description: Wheelchair-accessible minivan conversions with power ramps and lowered floors. Partners with Honda, Toyota, and Chrysler for factory-level accessible vehicle conversions.

name: PRC-Saltillo (Prentke Romich Company)
description: Leading manufacturer of AAC (augmentative and alternative communication) speech-generating devices and language systems for people who cannot speak. Used by SLPs and families worldwide.

name: AbleNet
description: Assistive technology switches, mounting systems, and adapted educational tools. Makes technology accessible for people with disabilities through simple, reliable devices.

name: National Ramp
description: Modular and portable wheelchair ramps for home access. Aluminum ramp systems that install quickly without concrete or permits. Rent or purchase options.

name: EZ-ACCESS
description: Portable and modular ramps, handrails, and access solutions for homes and buildings. Lightweight aluminum ramps for doorways, stairs, and vehicles.

name: Challenged Athletes Foundation
description: Provides grants for adaptive sports equipment and athletic training for people with physical disabilities. Funds handcycles, running prosthetics, sports wheelchairs, and more.

name: Christopher & Dana Reeve Foundation
description: Quality-of-life grants, peer mentoring, and the Paralysis Resource Center with free information specialists. Provides funding for adaptive equipment, home modifications, and community programs.

name: United Spinal Association
description: Advocacy, peer support, and resource referrals for people with spinal cord injuries and disorders. Publishes New Mobility magazine and operates a free resource center.

name: Love That Max
description: Award-winning blog and social media presence by Ellen Seidman, mom of Max who has cerebral palsy. Running since 2008, it informs, inspires, and helps parents of kids with disabilities with honest, widely-respected content.

name: Lucky Fin Project
description: 501(c)(3) nonprofit raising awareness and celebrating individuals with limb differences (symbrachydactyly, congenital, amputee). Creates parent support networks, links to medical resources, and hosts events.

name: The Able Fables (Instagram)
description: Dr. Nicole Julia, occupational therapist and inclusive children's book author. Shares disability inclusion content and promotes the Able Fables book series. 20% of profits donated to build inclusive playgrounds — over $60K donated.

name: Raising Disabled Podcast
description: Disability parenting podcast covering real experiences of raising children with disabilities. Offers community and shared stories for caregivers navigating the disability parenting journey.

name: Just Ask!: Be Different, Be Brave, Be You
description: By Sonia Sotomayor (Supreme Court Justice). Features children of various abilities planting a garden together, encouraging kids to ask questions about differences rather than make assumptions. Ages 3-8.

name: We're All Wonders
description: By R.J. Palacio. Companion to the bestselling Wonder. Tells the story of Auggie, an ordinary boy with an extraordinary face, encouraging readers to look past appearances and choose kindness. Ages 3-7.

name: El Deafo
description: By Cece Bell. Graphic memoir chronicling the author's childhood experience navigating the world with hearing loss using a Phonic Ear hearing aid. Told with humor and warmth. Newbery Honor book. Ages 8-12.

name: The Out-of-Sync Child
description: By Carol Stock Kranowitz. Explains Sensory Processing Differences (SPD) and presents a drug-free approach for parents. Helps parents recognize sensory issues and find appropriate therapies. A foundational text in the special needs parenting community.

name: How to Build a Thriving Marriage as You Care for Children with Disabilities
description: By Kristin Faith Evans and Todd Evans. Practical, evidence-based ways to protect and strengthen your marital relationship while navigating the demands of caring for children with disabilities. Published 2024.

name: Get Your Joy Back: Banishing Resentment and Reclaiming Confidence in Your Special Needs Family
description: By Laurie Wallin. Highlights forgiveness as the first step to improving daily life and shares practical strategies for addressing the 'wishing things were different' feelings that special needs parents experience.

name: Disability Visibility: First-Person Stories from the Twenty-First Century
description: Edited by Alice Wong. Anthology of essays by disabled writers covering Being, Becoming, Doing, and Connecting. An essential collection for understanding disability from diverse perspectives.

name: Laughing at My Nightmare
description: By Shane Burcaw. Memoir describing growing up with spinal muscular atrophy with humor and unflinching honesty. From the creator of Squirmy and Grubs.

name: The Accessible Home: Designing for All Ages and Abilities
description: By Deborah Pierce (architect). Comprehensive guide to creating accessible living environments using universal design principles — goes beyond basic ramps and grab bars to make homes accessible without sacrificing beauty or style.

name: Rules
description: By Cynthia Lord. Newbery Honor book featuring 12-year-old Catherine and her younger brother David who has autism. A touching story about acceptance and identity from the sibling perspective. Ages 9-12.

name: My Brother Charlie
description: By Holly Robinson Peete and Ryan Elizabeth Peete. Told from a twin sister's perspective about her brother Charlie who has autism. Shares what it's like to love, worry about, and understand a sibling with autism. Ages 4-8.

name: Being the Other One: Growing Up with a Brother or Sister Who Has Special Needs
description: By Kate Strohm. Explores the often-secret feelings of siblings based on the author's own experience and extensive interviews. Offers valuable coping strategies for teens and adults.

name: The Sibling Slam Book: What It's Really Like to Have a Brother or Sister with Special Needs
description: Compiled by Don Meyer. Real siblings answer questions about their experiences in a slam-book format. Honest, funny, and validating for teens who have a brother or sister with a disability.

name: Home Care Delivered (HCD)
description: Since 1996, HCD has delivered insurance-covered medical supplies including incontinence, diabetes, urology, and wound care products directly to your door. Dedicated pediatric/special needs program for children with autism, cerebral palsy, Down syndrome, spina bifida, and other conditions. Bills insurance and Medicaid directly — supplies at little to no cost.

name: Carewell
description: Online medical supply store founded by family caregivers, offering incontinence products, nutrition, wound care, and caregiving supplies. Customer care team includes nurses available 24/7. Accepts FSA/HSA cards. 30% off first Autoship subscription order.

name: Youth Crews
description: Founded by a father whose daughter was born with a brain injury, Youth Crews fills the gap between baby and adult diapers with age-appropriate incontinence products for kids ages 4-17 with disabilities. Soft, breathable tabbed diapers in sizes 9-11 with designs that aren't babyish or clinical. FSA/HSA eligible.

name: HDIS (Home Delivery Incontinence Supplies)
description: The nation's largest supplier of incontinence products since 1986. Carries major brands (Depend, Poise, TENA) with discreet home delivery. Accepts Medicaid. Free samples available. Only $2.95 flat order charge regardless of size, no shipping fees.

name: Aeroflow Urology
description: Provides incontinence supplies for adults and children through insurance with a simple 2-step eligibility process. Handles prescriptions, insurance navigation, and monthly shipments. Bills Medicaid, Medicare, BCBS, and many private plans directly — supplies can be 100% covered.

name: NorthShore Care Supply
description: Specializes in high-absorbency adult and youth diapers, bladder control pads, and waterproof bed pads. Dedicated special needs diapers section and youth/teen incontinence line. Founded by a Board Certified Patient Advocate. Has served over 2.5 million individuals. FSA/HSA accepted.

name: ActivStyle
description: Provides Medicaid-covered home medical supplies including incontinence products for adults and children. Dedicated pediatric program for children with autism, cerebral palsy, Down syndrome, developmental delays, and spina bifida. Handles doctor prescriptions on your behalf with discreet monthly delivery.

name: Parentgiving
description: Founded in 2008 by caregivers, offers premium incontinence products and caregiving supplies. Specific resources for individuals with Intellectual and Developmental Disabilities (IDD). Customer service led by an award-winning gerontologist with personal caregiving experience. FSA/HSA accepted.

name: S2 Medical Supply — Kids Corner
description: Provides catheters, ostomy, and incontinence supplies with a dedicated Kids Corner pediatric program for children with autism, special needs, and cerebral palsy. Team handles all paperwork and insurance coordination. Accepts Medicaid in select states (GA, AL, TX, SC). Free delivery.
